acceptance_criteria_proven: AC-W01-E04-S001-05 (added with the scope addition: a pristine scaffold's configs pass `wowapi config validate --env local` under the framework-only path)
revision_note: HEAD at execution; fix uncommitted on top (conductor commits). Working-tree delta for this task: internal/cli/templates/init/configs_base.yaml.tmpl (i18n block converted to a commented example; the file's combined diff also carries sibling W01-E03-S002's http timeout keys — disjoint hunks) + the new test in internal/cli/gen_crud_boots_test.go.
execution_environment: macOS Darwin 25.5.0 arm64, local dev workstation; test cwd internal/cli (no tools/configcheck) exercises exactly the framework-only fallback
result: PASS (post-fix); fail-first FAIL captured pre-fix with the exact i18n unknown-key rejections

runs.phase: fail-first (pre-fix, active i18n block in configs_base.yaml.tmpl)
runs.outcome: FAIL. Verbatim: "config: invalid configuration: i18n.default_locale: unknown key / i18n.go_bundles: unknown key / i18n.locales_dir: unknown key / i18n.supported_locales: unknown key" — reproducing W01-E04-S002 DEV-03(a) in-process.

PASS: `config validate --env local` on a pristine scaffold prints `config OK` + fingerprint. Fix: the scaffold's ACTIVE i18n block became a commented example, matching the file's own convention for product-owned sections (auth/security/storage/privileged/concurrency); the values shown were all compiled-in defaults except supported_locales, which nothing at runtime reads (appcfg I18nConfig zero value is documented valid; catalogs load from locales/ regardless; `wowapi i18n validate` takes --supported as a flag). Product behavior unchanged — TestInitI18nAcceptanceEndToEnd and every TestInitI18n*/TestInitConfigsBase* test still passes.
go test ./internal/cli/ -count=1 -> ok (13.0s) including TestGenCRUDOutputBoots, all i18n scaffold tests, and sibling stories' in-flight tests.

root_cause_note: True source of drift judged to be the scaffold template, not the schema: the i18n keys are product-owned (appcfg.Config.I18n; config.Framework cannot gain a duplicate `conf:"i18n"` section without colliding with every existing product's embedded-Framework composition), and the scaffold's own convention already ships every other product-owned/optional section commented. The DX-01 go.mod defect (which breaks the tools/configcheck delegation leg of `config validate` inside a scaffold) is a SEPARATE open defect, out of this task's scope — tracked by W01-E04-S001-T001.
